news.headline: DemokratieKioske Project Launches February 2026
news.summary: Lust auf besser leben gGmbH launched the DemokratieKioske project, a democracy engagement initiative running from February to September 2026. The project includes 30 Young Urban Future Labs in youth institutions (February-May) and 11 artistically redesigned kiosks across the Rhine-Main region hosting approximately 120 events (May-September). Funded by Hessische Landeszentrale für politische Bildung, Deutsche Fernsehlotterie, and Kultur-Fonds Frankfurt RheinMain.

news.headline: Bark Date event scheduled at Kiessee
news.summary: A Bark Date event was scheduled for January 31, 2026, from 11:00 to 13:00 at Wiese an der Westseite des Kiessees, featuring dogs from STREUNERHerzen animal welfare organization.

news.headline: Eintracht Frankfurt Partnership Discussions
news.summary: Daniela and Marlene from Lust auf besser leben gGmbH conducted partnership discussions with Eintracht Frankfurt last week as part of 2026 initiatives. The organization described the meetings as productive collaborative sessions.

news.headline: Crunchbase profiles BEEsharing in bee-based startups article
news.summary: Crunchbase News mentions BEEsharing.io as a Hamburg-based pollination brokerage network in a recent article on bee startups. It describes the platform connecting farmers, beekeepers, and vendors while handling logistics. Published within the last 30 days amid strong funding trends in the sector.

news.headline: Lendorse secures €10M debt capital line from Swiss i2 Group
news.summary: Munich-based Lendorse obtained a €10 million debt capital line from Swiss i2 Group to expand its Income Share Agreement business for international STEM students. The funding supports scaling student loans after prior €200K from Techstars, Vealo Ventures, and Earlybird. Founders are Björn Wolf and Evgenii Avdeev.

news.headline: ISB RLP invests in Nexol Photovolthermic AG
news.summary: ISB RLP (Investitions- und Strukturbank Rheinland-Pfalz), a German development bank, announced participation in Nexol Photovolthermic AG. This represents a concrete funding/investment event for the solar water heater startup.

news.headline: Zukunft Fahrrad und Radeln ohne Alter starten Kooperation
news.summary: Zukunft Fahrrad, Bundesverband der innovativen Fahrradwirtschaft, und Radeln ohne Alter Deutschland e.V. sind eine Kooperation eingegangen. Gemeinsam setzen sie sich für inklusive, nachhaltige Mobilität für alle Altersstufen und lebenswerte Städte ein.

news.headline: RoA Pocking erreicht über 80 Fördermitglieder
news.summary: Radeln ohne Alter Pocking wächst weiter mit über 80 Fördermitgliedern, mehr als die Hälfte sind geschulte Rikscha-Piloten. Die Flotte ermöglicht regelmäßige Fahrten für Senioren und Menschen mit Behinderungen.

news.headline: Takasago & Wachswandel win Social Entrepreneur Award Kreis Euskirchen 2025
news.summary: Takasago and Wachswandel received the Social Entrepreneur Award Kreis Euskirchen 2025 at a ceremonial event in the Ideenfabrik Nachhaltige Wirtschaft. The award recognizes their social entrepreneurial efforts in the Euskirchen district.

news.headline: Fainin receives fast track tickets and legal advice at Zebra Investment Night
news.summary: Fainin, a platform for renting rarely used items, participated in the premiere Zebra Investment Night by Impact Hub Hamburg. While other startups secured funding commitments, Fainin received legal advice and fast track tickets for further investor meetings. This event marked a successful debut with total funding commitments of 135,000 euros to four startups.

news.headline: Queermentor Role Model Awards 2026: 25 queer people and allies awarded in Berlin
news.summary: Queermentor awarded 25 queer individuals and allies, including Riccardo Simonetti and Armin Morbach, at the Role Model Awards 2026 held in Berlin. The event recognizes contributions to visibility, equal opportunities, and LGBTQIA+ empowerment in the workplace and society. This is the second edition following the first in January 2025.

news.headline: Bark Date event held in Ellwangen (Jagst)
news.summary: Bark Date organized a dog adoption event in Ellwangen on January 25, 2026, featuring up to 30 dogs from animal welfare organizations. Visitors could meet dogs wearing red bandanas marked 'adopt me' and schedule follow-up appointments for adoption discussions.

news.headline: AI.Land participates in Grüne Woche Berlin, present in two halls
news.summary: AI.Land is exhibiting at the 100th Grüne Woche in Berlin from January 16-25, 2026, showcasing agrarrobots Aplamo and DAVEGI in Halle 23 and NRW-Halle. The event highlights traditional vegetable farming revolutionized by robotics.

news.headline: BEEsharing participates in EIB Fado pitching challenge
news.summary: BEEsharing.io featured in the European Investment Bank's Fado pitching challenge. The event highlights the startup's pitch among innovative companies. This recognition underscores their role in agriculture and pollination innovation.

news.headline: biosafety4u.berlin GmbH and Cellcolab GmbH announce cooperation
news.summary: biosafety4u.berlin GmbH and Cellcolab GmbH announced a joint cooperation to support biotech startups with flexible lab spaces and regulatory services. The partnership provides one-stop-shop solutions for on-demand labs, equipment, and biosafety compliance to accelerate research. Cellcolab plans expansion to Berlin.

news.headline: Duni Group and Relevo launch ReMuc reusable container initiative in Munich
news.summary: Duni Group and Relevo by Duni launched the five-year ReMuc – 'Ois im Kreis' project at Munich's Viktualienmarkt, featuring automated return stations for reusable food containers. Relevo manages deposits, returns, and traceability digitally. Partners include Recup, Circle Cube, and Cup Company, aiming to reduce waste ahead of EU PPWR regulations.

news.headline: Duni Group partners with Relevo for Munich reusable food container system
news.summary: Duni Group partnered with majority-owned Relevo to trial a digital reusable container system at Viktualienmarkt over five years, installing five automated stations. The initiative promotes scalable reuse to cut packaging waste and vendor burdens. It aligns with upcoming EU Packaging and Packaging Waste Regulation.

news.headline: hellgrün Energie secures €1M growth capital from Saxovent
news.summary: Cologne-based hellgrün Energie GmbH raised 1 million euros in growth financing from Saxovent Smart Eco Investments GmbH to accelerate rollout of onsite PV solutions for commercial real estate in retail and logistics. The funding supports scaling decarbonization efforts without owners becoming energy suppliers. hellgrün plans over 100 MW capacity by 2027.

news.headline: hellgrün Energie gets Wachstumskapital from Saxovent
news.summary: hellgrün Energie received 1 million euros growth financing from Saxovent to expand PV pipeline for commercial properties including retail, logistics, and manufacturing. Supported by angel investors like Tobias Dollberg, the funding targets scalable decarbonization of rooftops.

news.headline: Saxovent expands stake in hellgrün Energie with growth capital
news.summary: Saxovent increased its investment in hellgrün Energie, providing additional growth capital and becoming the second-largest shareholder after founders. The funding enables scaling of PV projects on commercial real estate.

news.headline: r3leaf participates in HTWK Leipzig startup event at CCL
news.summary: r3leaf, with HTWK alumnus Tore Waldhausen, is featured among startups at a research conference on January 20, 2026, at Congress Center Leipzig. The event highlights innovative projects from the university. This marks public recognition of the company's involvement in the local tech ecosystem.

news.headline: Radeln ohne Alter e.V. startet Aktivitäten in Bonn
news.summary: Radeln ohne Alter e.V. wird nun auch in Bonn aktiv mit Rikscha-Fahrten für Menschen mit eingeschränkter Mobilität.

news.headline: Lufthansa Industry Solutions partners with SPRK.global on food waste platform
news.summary: Lufthansa Industry Solutions is supporting the Berlin-based greentech startup SPRK.global in developing a global digital trading platform to prevent food surpluses and promote resource conservation. This strategic cooperation aims to build technology for reducing food waste in the supply chain. The partnership is highlighted in LHIND's recent news overview.

news.headline: Gelzhäuser Forst siblings win Waldpreis award for forestry innovation
news.summary: Lisa and Timo Gelzhäuser from Gelzhäuser Forst in Kierspe received the Waldpreis, awarded in Berlin, for innovative forestry practices after climate damage and bark beetle infestations forced them to pivot to building and selling Tiny Houses from affected wood. The award highlights their efforts in reforestation, biodiversity, and direct wood marketing. They plan to invest the 2000 Euro prize in reforestation information.

news.headline: Manifesta 16 Ruhr and lala.ruhr to host market event at St. Josef church
news.summary: Manifesta 16 Ruhr and lala.ruhr will transform the former St. Josef church in Gelsenkirchen into a market on February 28, 2026. They are seeking creators from the Ruhr Area for projects in upcycled crafts, handmade design, and visual arts. This is part of the pre-biennial warm-up programme.

news.headline: DBU awards Nunos GmbH 125,000 Euro grant for odorless manure fertilizer tech
news.summary: The Deutsche Bundesstiftung Umwelt (DBU) is funding startup Nunos from Hürth with 125,000 Euros to develop a biological process converting manure into efficient, odorless fertilizer. The technology, spun out from DLR space research, reduces greenhouse gas emissions and improves nutrient availability, with field trials planned for 2026. Co-founder Tim Paulke highlighted up to 20% yield increases expected.

news.headline: eco:fibr announces next operational milestone for toll production in 2026
news.summary: eco:fibr shared a blog post on their next operational milestone for toll production targeting 2026. This aligns with their crowdinvesting campaign to fund ton-scale production and market entry at the start of 2026. The update highlights progress toward pilot production.

news.headline: meine ernte launches new products at Hornbach
news.summary: meine ernte expanded its product portfolio with new offerings specifically designed for plant cultivation. The company started an exclusive cooperation with Hornbach to expand the range of nature-based gardening products.

news.headline: Munevo raises €3M for US expansion
news.summary: Munevo GmbH completed a €3 million financing round from Bavaria Capital, BayBG, Be10x Capital, Roca X, and other investors. The funds will support US market expansion, sales network growth, and European partnerships. CEO Claudiu Leverenz highlighted the impact on helping more people with head-controlled wheelchairs.

news.headline: Cozero raises €6.5M in Series A funding
news.summary: Berlin-based Cozero, a carbon accounting software startup founded in 2020, secured €6.5 million in Series A funding. The investment supports growth in sustainability software for emissions management.

news.headline: Goodgive places 2nd in Rheinland-Pitch Halbfinale Köln Female Founders Edition
news.summary: Goodgive participated in the Rheinland-Pitch Halbfinale Köln Female Founders Edition and secured second place after As Good As Pros. The event, organized with Female Innovation Hub, IHK Köln, and Synnous Consulting, featured pitches to qualify for the summer finale. Goodgive offers eco-friendly reusable fabric gift wrapping.

news.headline: inGemeinschaft wins Gründerpreis from Stadtsparkasse München
news.summary: The Munich startup inGemeinschaft, a platform matching people with spare rooms to students seeking affordable housing, won the Startup category of the Gründerpreis from Stadtsparkasse München. The award recognizes its sustainable intergenerational housing concept that creates new living space without construction and promotes social interaction. The platform offers free profiles, intelligent matching, transparency, and personal support.

news.headline: KEY2BE.ME selected among 12 startups for Lübeck future program
news.summary: KEY2BE.ME was chosen as one of twelve startups in Lübeck to be prepared for the future through a regional program announced by Wirtschaftsminister Dr. Bernd Buchholz. The initiative highlights promising companies from Schleswig-Holstein, including Kiel and Lübeck. KEY2BE.ME develops a career orientation community with online events and a personal guide app.

news.headline: mySHOEFITTER wins jury and audience at Dortmund University Days
news.summary: mySHOEFITTER from Hamm most convinced the jury and audience with their presentation at the Dortmund University Days in January 2026. The event featured various short announcements and notes. This recognition highlights the startup's appeal in a competitive university setting.

news.headline: Nevalu's pop-up store project wins Marketing Prize Münster-Osnabrück
news.summary: The project 'P41 | REACH goes Prinzipalmarkt x Nevalu' received the Marketing Prize from Marketing Club Münster-Osnabrück for innovative marketing connecting education, innovation, science, and retail. The 6-week pop-up store attracted over 26,000 visitors and highlighted sustainability, recycling, and innovation. It was a collaboration with REACH EUREGIO and P41 initiator Tobias Viehoff.

news.headline: fobizz acquires Stuttgart-based edtech startup to teach
news.summary: Hamburg-based edtech startup fobizz has taken over to teach from Stuttgart, making it a subsidiary of 101 skills GmbH. Founders Felix Weiß and Marius Lindenmeier remain managing directors. The merger integrates to teach's AI tools for personalized teaching materials into fobizz's platform, reaching more teachers globally.

news.headline: eeden closes €18M Series A funding round led by Forbion
news.summary: German startup eeden, based in Münster, completed an €18M Series A funding round led by Forbion's BioEconomy Fund, with participation from new investors Henkel Ventures and NRW.Venture, plus existing backers like HTGF and TechVision Fund. The funds will build a demonstration plant in Münster, optimize large-scale textile recycling processing, and launch commercial projects with industry partners. This follows successful pilot plant validation.

news.headline: Nexol launches NEX RS series hybrid solar water heater
news.summary: Nexol Photovolthermic released the NEX RS series, a new hybrid solar water heater for residential applications available in four sizes (80-300 liters) with 1.5 kW heating output. The plug-and-play system starts at €700 and represents a major product expansion beyond previous offerings.

news.headline: Mental Hub wins 3rd place in Campus Competition Cologne C3 2025
news.summary: Mental Hub secured third place in the C3 2025 pitch finale at InnoDom Cologne, competing against other Gateway Hochschule Köln startups. The team impressed the jury with their evidence-based peer-support platform developed with research institutions and experts. They are eligible for investment offers from the total 300,000 Euro prize pool.

news.headline: S2S Dynamics participates in Impact Accelerator final pitch in Hamburg
news.summary: S2S Dynamics was one of eight startups that completed the 11-week Impact Accelerator program by Impact Hub Hamburg, focused on sustainability and circular economy. The company presented its AI-powered electronic waste recycling solution at the final pitch event. A jury was impressed, though BuildlinX won for building decarbonization software.

news.headline: SIRPLUS food rescue startup gets rescue and fresh start
news.summary: Berlin-based SIRPLUS relaunches with new partnerships to optimize costs and return to roots. Co-founders expand portfolio to include rescued household items and wellness products from a single Berlin-Weißensee source. Subscription-surprise model continues alongside individual orders.

news.headline: Kiwifalter selected as venue for COWORK2026 conference
news.summary: Kiwifalter in Düsseldorf-Kaiserswerth has been chosen as the location partner for the COWORK2026 conference and barcamp, running April 23-25, 2026. The event will host the German Coworking Federation's annual conference with focus on diversity in coworking and includes workshops for founders.

news.headline: Ukrainian Hacker School event: Home security system workshop
news.summary: Hacker School hosts Ukrainian-language workshop for children 10-15 using micro:bit to build and program a home security system prototype at Ukraine Haus in Hamburg.

news.headline: Lendorse secures up to €10M in debt capital
news.summary: Lendorse, a Munich startup offering ISA financing for students, secured €10M debt capital from i2 Group as reported by Finance Forward. The funds will scale student loans beyond the initial €200K tested. Founders Björn Wolf and Evgenii Avdeev lead the 2022-founded company.

news.headline: Kiwifalter hosts children's art exhibition at Kaiserswerth Library
news.summary: Kiwifalter, a family-focused creative and professional development organization in Düsseldorf-North, is displaying children's artistic illustrations at Bücherei Kaiserswerth. The free exhibition runs from February 26 to April 15, 2026, showcasing creative works produced by children at the organization.

news.headline: Rainer den Ouden continues leading Adconterra as ADCONIA management changes
news.summary: ADCONIA, parent of Adconterra in Oberhausen, announced a management transition where founder Gregor van Ackeren hands over to Rainer den Ouden and Oliver Kreienbrink. Rainer den Ouden remains managing partner of sustainability subsidiary Adconterra alongside Sinja Krauskopf, focusing on ESG strategies. This ensures continuity for Adconterra's operations amid ADCONIA's leadership refresh.

news.headline: fleXality to Present at fish international 2026
news.summary: fleXality is participating in the 20th fish international trade fair at MESSE BREMEN from February 22-24, 2026. Managing Director Sören Eilenberger will present on AI and energy efficiency in cold storage on February 25, demonstrating how frozen fish can be used as energy storage to save costs and CO₂.

news.headline: CRISP publishes needs analysis results for conflict simulator
news.summary: CRISP released results from a needs analysis survey of youth work professionals on conflict challenges. 60% reported increased conflicts often fueled by social media; 74% open to VR simulation training. Findings will inform development of AI-supported VR conflict simulator.

news.headline: Modell-Standorte für Postcode-Lotterie-Projekt feststehen
news.summary: Radeln ohne Alter hat die Modell-Standorte für das geförderte Projekt 'Mit frischem Wind gegen die Einsamkeit: Radelnd Generationen verbinden' festgelegt. Das Projekt von der Deutschen Postcode Lotterie finanziert, zielt auf Ehrenamtsengagement junger Menschen (16-30 Jahre) ab, um Einsamkeit zu bekämpfen.

news.headline: Neue Kooperation mit Zukunft Fahrrad für inklusive Mobilität
news.summary: Radeln ohne Alter Deutschland e.V. und Zukunft Fahrrad, Bundesverband der innovativen Fahrradwirtschaft, sind eine Kooperation eingegangen. Gemeinsam setzen sie sich für inklusive, nachhaltige Mobilität und gesellschaftliche Teilhabe ein.

news.startup_name: ReDI School of Digital Integration gGmbH
news.headline: ReDI Talent Summit scheduled for July 21, 2026 in Munich
news.summary: ReDI School announced its Summer Talent Summit 2026, a flagship recruitment event in Munich where companies can meet recent graduates from advanced tech programs. The event aims to connect employers seeking to diversify their workforce with skilled ReDI talents trained in front-end, back-end, data analysis, UX/UI design, cloud, and computer science.

news.headline: PROSERVATION launches international newsletter
news.summary: PROSERVATION announced the launch of its international newsletter on February 17, 2026. The English e-paper will be available from issue 2/26 in May 2026. This represents the company's expansion of its communication and outreach efforts.

news.headline: Repair Rebels wins Deutscher Nachhaltigkeitspreis 2026
news.summary: Repair Rebels received the German Sustainability Award 2026. The company expressed being overwhelmed by the recognition. This marks a significant accolade for their fashion repair platform.

news.headline: Repair Rebels partners with Hessnatur and WeDress Collective
news.summary: Repair Rebels is collaborating with Germany's oldest sustainable fashion brand Hessnatur and fashion rental startup WeDress Collective. The partnerships leverage their technology and craftsmen network to offer repair services. Expansion from Düsseldorf pilot to Cologne is underway.

news.headline: Hacker School announces online info sessions for Girls' Day 2026
news.summary: Hacker School is seeking companies for Girls' Day on April 23, 2026, to inspire girls in IT through practical courses. Online info sessions scheduled for February 10 at 9 AM and February 26 at 11 AM.

news.headline: Plastic Fischer loads 16,000 kg of river plastic in Bangalore, India
news.summary: Plastic Fischer team in Bangalore loaded over 16,000 kg of collected river plastic onto a truck for processing at their Material Recovery Facility. This operational milestone highlights ongoing river cleanup efforts, including community engagement and preventing plastic from reaching lakes used for water supply. The VLOG documents activities at deployment sites and sorting processes.

news.headline: Africa GreenTec CEO Wolfgang Rams resigns after one year
news.summary: CEO Wolfgang Rams, who replaced founder Torsten Schreiber in January 2024, has left the company as of December 2025. Ulf Brackmann is now listed as the new CEO since January 2026. The company issued no press release and did not respond to inquiries about the leadership change.

news.headline: SNOWDANCE collaborates with Manifesta 16 Ruhr and lala.ruhr
news.summary: lala.ruhr is involved in the SNOWDANCE meets Manifesta 16 Ruhr event, a warm-up programme featuring migrant self-empowerment through music and film. The event includes conversations with actress Jasmina Musić and curator Gürsoy Doğtaş. It highlights cultural programming in Gelsenkirchen.

news.headline: Lendorse secures €10M debt financing from i2 Group
news.summary: Munich-based Lendorse secured a €10 million debt capital line from Swiss i2 Group to expand its Income Share Agreement model for international students. The funding enables financing for over 1,000 talented students from developing countries to study in Europe. Previous equity funding totaled €1.75M in June 2025 and €200K from Techstars, Vealo Ventures, and Earlybird.

news.headline: Mindset Technologies wins Austrian Armed Forces hackathon
news.summary: Mindset Technologies AG won a hackathon organized by the Austrian Armed Forces. The event took place in Grosshöflein, Austria. This represents a concrete competitive achievement and recognition in the technology sector.

news.headline: Hejmo Homes Musterhaus listed on Airbnb for rentals
news.summary: Hejmo Homes' 33 m² sustainable tiny modular house in Grimma is now available as an Airbnb rental listing. The property is described as particularly energy-efficient. This marks a new commercialization step for their model home.

news.headline: Polycare expects product approval from German Institute in May 2026
news.summary: Polycare, a Thuringia-based startup, anticipates regulatory approval of its sustainable building stone products from the Deutsches Institut für Bautechnik (German Institute for Building Technology) in May 2026. The company produces reusable building materials for DIY sustainable homes that reduce CO2 emissions and address sand scarcity.

news.headline: r3leaf recognized as German AI sustainability solution
news.summary: r3leaf was identified as a German-made AI specializing in sustainability assessment in the construction industry. The company is among notable German startups developing AI tools for evaluating building sustainability metrics and environmental impact.

news.headline: AKDB spendet 5.000 Euro an Community Kitchen München
news.summary: Die AKDB hat der Community Kitchen München 5.000 Euro im Rahmen ihrer CSR-Initiative gespendet. Die Übergabe fand in den Räumen der Kitchen in Neuperlach statt. Gründerin Günes Seyfarth betonte den Beitrag zum Klimaschutz durch Lebensmittelrettung.

news.headline: eco:fibr launches crowdinvesting campaign on FunderNation
news.summary: Hanover-based greentech startup eco:fibr GmbH launched a crowdinvesting campaign on FunderNation, allowing investments from 100 euros. Funds will finance ton-scale production start and market entry in early 2026, plus pilot production in Costa Rica. The campaign supports sustainable pulp production from pineapple plant waste without harmful chemicals.

news.headline: Fainin participates in Impact Accelerator final pitch in Hamburg
news.summary: Fainin, offering a platform for renting sporadically used items, was one of eight startups that completed the 11-week Impact Accelerator program focused on sustainability and circular economy. The program provided mentoring and culminated in a final pitch event judged by experts. BuildlinX won, but all participants including Fainin presented their progress.

news.headline: guud GmbH nominated for Deutscher Nachhaltigkeitspreis 2025
news.summary: guud GmbH from München has been nominated for the Deutscher Nachhaltigkeitspreis 2025 for its sustainable employee benefits like guudcard and guudschein. The award, Europe's largest with around 800 applicants, recognizes efforts to promote conscious lifestyles among employees. This nomination highlights guud's impact on environment, social responsibility, and fair supply chains.

news.headline: icho systems raises angel investment from German investors
news.summary: icho systems, a Duisburg, Germany-based healthcare manufacturer of dementia products, secured an angel investment round. The funding was provided by German angel investors including Simon Krivec and Joachim Vollenschier. The company, founded in 2018 by CEO Steffen Preuss, develops technology-supported therapy balls for dementia patients.

news.headline: LUUP Coffee shuts down online shop due to founder's personal reasons
news.summary: LUUP Coffee, a lupin-based coffee alternative startup from Düsseldorf, closed its online shop on February 1, 2026. The decision was made by founder Christian for personal reasons. The company continues to promote its sustainable, caffeine-free, acid-poor lupin coffee product.

news.headline: Organifarms gains EBZ Group as strategic partner and investor
news.summary: Constance-based Organifarms secured EBZ Group as a strategic partner and investor to support product development, growth, and scaling of strawberry harvesting robots. EBZ will aid in achieving industrial-level production reliability. The robots are already in use by initial customers.

news.headline: BLLV becomes cooperation partner of Hacker School in Bavaria
news.summary: The Bavarian Teachers' Association (BLLV) partners with Hacker School to bring free programming courses to schools across Bavaria, supported by TÜV SÜD-Stiftung. Aims to introduce students to IT careers.

news.headline: Hacker School exhibiting at didacta Köln March 10-14
news.summary: Hacker School presents free online programming courses for schools at didacta trade fair in Cologne, Hall 07.1 Stand E011, demonstrating 21st century skills training.

news.headline: ZamBam Sports launches crowdfunding to boost shoe production in Zambia
news.summary: ZamBam Sports is initiating a crowdfunding campaign to stimulate shoe production in Zambia. The effort aims to enhance manufacturing of shoe soles and related products. Reported in local Ingolstadt news segment.

news.headline: ArthroTarget wins Publikumspreis at UDE startup event
news.summary: ArthroTarget received the audience award (Publikumspreis) at a University of Duisburg-Essen startup competition, celebrated with applause alongside GreeNNest. The team benefits from EXIST-Gründungsstipendium funding, workshops on business models, financing, and sales. The company focuses on DACH region expansion and plans entry into Scandinavia and other European markets.

news.headline: Organifarms becomes GmbH, moves to new offices in Constance
news.summary: Organifarms transitioned to a limited liability company (GmbH), enabling eligibility for the INVEST Venture Capital Grant from BAFA. The team relocated to new offices at their farm in Constance, Germany, providing space for R&D and production expansion.

news.headline: Westdeutsche Zeitung reports on Inklupreneur Rheinland Starter Camp in Wuppertal
news.summary: The Westdeutsche Zeitung covered the two-day kickoff event of the Inklupreneur Rheinland 2026 program in Wuppertal, a cooperation with found it = e.V. Eight companies participated in this LVR-funded initiative to help startups integrate inclusion for disabled employees from the start.

news.headline: Du bist hier der Chef! ends operations after six years
news.summary: The consumer initiative Du bist hier der Chef! ceased operations at the end of 2025 after six years of activity. The organization faced significant challenges with retail partners who were hesitant to implement higher purchasing prices and price transparency. However, the flagship Verbraucher-Milch (consumer milk) product will continue to be available in already-supplied stores, and the initiative will transition to volunteer-led operations.

news.headline: Lust auf besser leben gGmbH launches DemokratieKioske project for WDC 2026
news.summary: The company announced the DemokratieKioske project, opening a new chapter in democratic participation as part of World Design Capital 2026. Official launch event set for January 22, 2026, featuring future sites and over 30 artists for 120 events. Press release confirms involvement in promoting sustainability and participation.

news.headline: Luma Life joins IFB InnoFounder program for dementia app
news.summary: Luma Life received admission to the IFB Innovationsstarter GmbH InnoFounder program in November 2025. The funding supports the launch of their AI-based voice assistant app that aids dementia patients with daily reminders, conversations, cognitive games, and family health updates. This enables the startup to advance product development amid rising dementia challenges in aging societies.

news.headline: capacura invests in MOOT Upcycling GmbH
news.summary: Cologne-based impact investor capacura GmbH announced an investment in Berlin-based upcycling startup MOOT Upcycling GmbH, founded in 2020. The funding supports MOOT's sustainable fashion from discarded textiles and upcycling services for companies like DHL and DFB.

news.headline: Alon Akademie opens innovative learning space in Wesel
news.summary: Alon Akademie launched a new learning location in Wesel for children and youth focused on discovery and hands-on making. The facility represents an expansion of educational initiatives in the region, supported by ALTANA AG.

news.headline: Maluma presents digital education platform at Berlin Startup Pitch 2025
news.summary: Maluma participated in the Berlin Startup Pitch 2025 on March 20, presenting its digital education platform to high-calibre investors. The event featured 20 selected Berlin startups showcasing innovative ideas, highlighting Berlin's role as a startup hub. This pitch opportunity positions Maluma for potential investor interest.

news.headline: WhiteWall becomes new partner of Art Meets Education
news.summary: WhiteWall has partnered with Art Meets Education to support the NGO's initiatives. This strategic partnership aims to aid in financing education through photography projects for underprivileged children. The collaboration was announced in a recent news update.

news.headline: CEBRA Talent Accepted into IFB InnoImpact Funding Program
news.summary: CEBRA Talent was accepted into IFB Innovationsstarter GmbH's InnoImpact funding program in October 2024. The startup, founded by Livia Martins and Marielen Winter, focuses on transforming young talents in companies into impact intrapreneurs to drive forward a regenerative economy through on-the-job training programs.
